title: Top K Frequent Elements
problem_statement: Return the k most frequent elements from an array.

examples.explanation: 1 and 2 are the two most frequent elements.

constraints: k is in the range [1, number of unique elements]
intuition: Count element frequencies and use a heap to maintain the k most frequent elements efficiently.

approaches.name: Frequency map + min-heap
approaches.description: Build a frequency map and keep a min-heap of size k keyed by frequency, then extract the heap contents.
approaches.why_not: Sorting all unique elements is O(n log n); heap-based selection is faster when k is small.

interview_qa.q: Why use a heap of size k?
interview_qa.a: It keeps only the top k frequent elements and avoids storing more than necessary.

interview_qa.q: How do you choose the heap key?
interview_qa.a: Use frequency as the comparison key so the least frequent among the top k is easy to remove.
